title: When Predictive Coding Oscillates

runtime.expected_outcome: eta * (pi_1 + pi_2) > 2 makes the sweep oscillate or diverge; shrinking eta stabilises it.

audiences.kid: If you change your mind too hard, you overshoot. Slow down, get it right.
audiences.real: Oscillation in PC is a standard gradient-descent failure: eta * Lipschitz > 2. Lower eta or pi until stability.
audiences.equation: For quadratic F with gradient Lipschitz L ~ pi_1 + pi_2, eta < 2/L is required for convergence.
audiences.derivation: Standard result from gradient descent on quadratic functions (Nesterov 2004). Oscillation is an early warning that the system is near its stability boundary.

orchestrate.objective: Diagnose oscillation in predictive-coding sweeps and fix it.
orchestrate.context: PredictiveCodingPass with eta sweeping through {0.05, 0.3, 0.7, 1.2}; pi_1+pi_2=2.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 5.
Nesterov (2004), Introductory Lectures on Convex Optimization.
